title: ApiKey newtype implements Serialize that emits raw secret defeating redaction guarantees
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/providers/api_key.rs:173-177
## Violation
ApiKey's Serialize impl writes the raw value:

  impl Serialize for ApiKey {
      fn serialize(&self, serializer) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
          serializer.serialize_str(&self.0)
      }
  }

This defeats the redacting Debug/Display contract advertised by the module doc. Any code path that serializes a ProviderConfig (e.g., to a debug log, to a session-persist JSON, to a tracing field value) leaks the raw secret. The hash-not-implemented and copy-not-implemented design choices show real care, but Serialize is documented as the safe boundary while in practice it is the leakage point. The existing test serde_roundtrip_ok actually asserts the leak ('sk-ant-api03-valid-test-value' serializes verbatim).
## Mandated refactor
Either (a) Serialize emits the redacted form (breaks YAML config roundtrip — explicitly fine because config is loaded once and never written back by the application), or (b) gate raw Serialize behind a dedicated wrapper UnredactedApiKey<'a>(&'a ApiKey) that callers must opt in to. Add a regression test that asserts ANY serialization of a ProviderConfig containing an ApiKey does NOT contain the raw secret.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
